summary.text: Lake Monticello, SC is a hydroelectric reservoir about 6,700 surface acres, with a maximum depth near 89 feet. Confirmed sport fish include Largemouth Bass, Smallmouth Bass, Spotted Bass, Bluegill, Redear Sunfish Shellcracker, Warmouth, Black Crappie, Blue Catfish, Channel Catfish, Flathead Catfish, White Bass / Hybrid, White Perch. Habitat facts currently confirm 12 mapped fish attractors; mapped structure includes channelLedges, humps, flats.

_extractedFacts.fact: The reservoir is impounded by four earthen dams (A, B, C, and D).

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Monticello was completed in 1978.
_extractedFacts.quote: Since their completion in 1978, Monticello Reservoir and its adjacent Recreationa l Lake (hereto called the “Recreational Lake”), together with the previously existing Par Reservoir have become popular recreational sites in western Fairfield County, South Carolina.

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Monticello covers 6,700 acres.

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Monticello is owned and operated by South Carolina Electric & Gas Company (SCE&G).
_extractedFacts.quote: All these impoundments are owned and operated by South Carolina Electric & Gas Company (herein called the “Company” or SCE&G) pursuant to a license issued by the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (herein called the “FERC”).

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Monticello experiences daily water level fluctuations of 4.5 to 5 feet.
_extractedFacts.quote: Monticello drops 4.5 to 5 feet over a 10 to 12 hour period during the generating phase.

_extractedFacts.quote: The Recreational Lake adjacent to Monticello Reservoir has a surface area of 300 acres which is maintained at a stable water level and is not affected by the operation of the pumped storage facility.

_extractedFacts.fact: The Recreational Lake (sub-impoundment) maintains a stable water level.
_extractedFacts.quote: The Recreational Lake adjacent to Monticello Reservoir has a surface area of 300 acres which is maintained at a stable water level and is not affected by the operation of the pumped storage facility.

_extractedFacts.quote: A smaller Monticello Sub-Impoundment (The Recreational Lake) is located north of the large body of water and is about 300 acres in size with 10.2 miles of shoreline.

_extractedFacts.fact: E. coli levels in Jackson Cove were found to be four times higher than recommended levels for safe swimming.
_extractedFacts.source: E. coli levels high in part of Lake Monticello
_extractedFacts.quote: Results came back showing E. coli levels four times higher than the recommended levels for safe swimming in Jackson Cove, according to one member of the association.

_extractedFacts.fact: The Lake Monticello Owners Association (LMOA) advised that swimming is prohibited in Jackson Cove due to high E. coli levels.
_extractedFacts.source: E. coli levels high in part of Lake Monticello
_extractedFacts.quote: "Lake Monticello Owners Association, or LMOA, they are still advising that no one can swim in Jackson Cove,"

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Monticello is one of four South Carolina reservoirs where White Perch were collected for growth dynamics analysis.
_extractedFacts.source: 2017 Fisheries Investigations in Lakes and Streams
_extractedFacts.quote: Number of observations was 57, 20, 22, and 61 from lakes Wateree, Santee-Cooper, Murray, and Monticello, respectively.

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Monticello is approximately 6,000 acres in size.
_extractedFacts.quote: Monticello is a fairly small (about 6,000 acres) impoundment

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Monticello experiences daily water level fluctuations due to its operation as a pumped storage reservoir.
_extractedFacts.quote: that goes through daily water level fluctuations due to its operation as a bump back storage reservoir.

_extractedFacts.fact: Nongame fishing devices are prohibited in Lake Monticello to manage the lake for recreational fishing only.
_extractedFacts.quote: More importantly, the SCDNR wants to manage Lake Monticello for recreational fishing only.

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Monticello and Parr Reservoir are connected by a pumped storage system.
_extractedFacts.quote: Are not the Parr Reservoir and Lake Monticello adjoining reservoirs which are connected by a fish grinder, AKA pumped storage?

_extractedFacts.fact: In July, most black bass in Lake Monticello are found in deeper water off points and humps.
_extractedFacts.source: SCDNR - News Releases - Freshwater Fishing Trends
_extractedFacts.quote: In July most of the fish will be in deeper water off points and humps

_extractedFacts.fact: Effective lures for black bass in Lake Monticello during July include soft plastics, flutter spoons, or big crankbaits.
_extractedFacts.source: SCDNR - News Releases - Freshwater Fishing Trends
_extractedFacts.quote: soft plastics, flutter spoons, or big crankbaits will be the best way to catch them

_extractedFacts.fact: Black bass fishing in Lake Monticello is generally better when there is current.
_extractedFacts.source: SCDNR - News Releases - Freshwater Fishing Trends
_extractedFacts.quote: Overall, the fishing is usually better when there is current.

_extractedFacts.fact: In July, catfish in Lake Monticello can be caught by free-line drifting over deep water or anchoring cut bait on humps.
_extractedFacts.source: SCDNR - News Releases - Freshwater Fishing Trends
_extractedFacts.quote: numbers of fish will be caught free-line drifting over deep water, while bigger fish can be caught anchoring cut bait on humps.

_extractedFacts.fact: A storm sewer overflow was identified as a potential cause for the high E. coli levels in the lake.
_extractedFacts.source: E. coli levels high in part of Lake Monticello
_extractedFacts.quote: The LMOA says it could have been caused by storm sewer overflow.
